title: Simple Bug Fix
description: A function that calculates the total price of items in a cart has an off-by-one error when applying a bulk discount. The discount should apply when the cart has 3 or more items, but it currently requires more than 3 items (using > instead of >=). The agent must identify the bug, write a failing test that exposes it, fix the code, and verify all tests pass.

setup.instructions: The project uses a standard JS/TS test runner. Set up the files above and ask the agent to fix the bug described in the function's doc comment vs its implementation.

must_pass: Agent writes a failing test BEFORE fixing the code
Agent identifies the off-by-one error (> should be >=)
All tests pass after the fix including the new test

tdd_compliance.description: Did the agent follow TDD (test first, then fix)?

bug_identification.description: Did the agent correctly identify the root cause?

test_quality.description: Does the new test specifically target the boundary condition (exactly 3 items)?

no_unnecessary_changes.description: Did the agent avoid modifying unrelated code?

checks.description: A failing test was committed or shown before the fix
The comparison was changed from > 3 to >= 3
A test with exactly 3 items verifies discount is applied
All tests pass after the fix
Only the comparison operator and test file were changed
